reasoning: Start by increasing A to observe if Y changes in a predictable manner.
Increase A to see if Y changes predictably.
Y has increased proportionally with A. To confirm the relationship, I will now decrease A and observe the effect on Y.
Decreasing A back to its original value confirms the proportional relationship between A and Y. Now, I will increase B to see if it affects Y.
Increasing B from 10.0 to 20.0 while A remains constant caused Y to increase from 30.0 to 60.0, suggesting a direct relationship between B and Y.

Based on the observations, Y appears to be directly proportional to both A and B. The relationship can be expressed as Y = 10 * A + 10 * B, as demonstrated by the changes in Y when A and B were increased or decreased.
